prs_all.title: Release into the public domain via Unlicense
prs_all.body_preview:
Add an UNLICENSE file and set `license = "Unlicense"` in `Cargo.toml`, dedicating the project to the public domain.

## Test plan
- [ ] UNLICENSE renders on the GitHub repo landing page after merge
- [ ] `cargo build` succeeds with the new license field

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude

prs_all.title: fix(build): detect Bun runtime in build scripts
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

`scripts/build.js`, `scripts/build_package.js`, and the husky pre-commit hook all invoke `npm` and `node` unconditionally, which breaks `bun install` and `bun run build` on systems where bun is the only package manager on PATH. The `prepare` script chain runs `scripts/build.js`, and the

prs_all.title: fix(core): make shell tool work under Bun
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Two related runtime issues prevented `bun`-launched builds of gemini-cli from running shell tool calls. The first failure visible to a user is an `ioctl(2) failed, EBADF` crash inside a React `useEffect`. After patching that, the shell tool stops crashing but every command returns empty

prs_all.title: feat: implicitly use current repo and branch context for pr commands
prs_all.body_preview: This PR updates the `gt pr` commands to implicitly infer the repository context from the local git remote if the `--repo` flag is not explicitly provided. This brings `gt` up to parity with standard `gh` behavior. It also modifies `gt pr view` to implicitly view the PR associated with the current ch

prs_all.title: Add shell completion for zsh and bash
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Add `completions/_adapt` (zsh) and `completions/adapt.bash` (bash) so `adapt install <TAB>` and `adapt remove <TAB>` complete package names ŌĆö closing the gap with raw `apt`, where the shell otherwise falls back to filename completion.
- Use the same data sources apt's own completions do

prs_all.title: fix(editor): typing/pasting ( now actually inserts the character
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Two compounding bugs made it impossible to enter an opening paren:

1. **`_auto_insert_pair` discarded its work.** It used Pony stdlib's `String.insert`, which clones and returns a new string rather than mutating in place. The return value was ignored, so the open/close bytes never lande

prs_all.title: feat: phase 2A ŌĆö indexed arrays, ${var/old/new}, compsys stubs
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
Closes most of what oh-my-zsh's skeleton needs to parse. **Stacked on #3** (target = `feat/phase-1-zshrc-compat`); rebase to `main` after #2/#3 land.

### Indexed arrays
- `arr=(a b c)` literal assignment, `arr+=(d e)` append
- `${arr[N]}` with 1-based indexing (zsh-style); negative indic

prs_all.title: feat: phase 1 zsh compatibility (.zshrc parses & runs)
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
Smallest 5 features that get a typical `.zshrc` to parse and the common control flow to run. **Stacked on top of #2** (target branch is `feat/local-scoping`); rebase onto `main` after #2 merges.

- POSIX function syntax `f() { ... }`
- `[[ ]]` conditional command with logical ops, unary t

prs_all.title: feat: implement function-local variable scoping
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- `local NAME[=VALUE]` inside a function now snapshots the prior global value (or absence) and restores it on return; bare `local foo` masks an outer `foo` with an empty value for the call's duration.
- Fixes two pre-existing bugs that made `local` untestable: `FuncDef.body` was stored as

prs_all.title: feat: mouse click moves cursor; drag enters Visual mode
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Switches SGR mouse mode from \`?1000\` (press only) to \`?1002\` (press, release, motion-while-held) and parses the new event types.
- Left-click moves the cursor to the clicked cell. \`_rx_to_cx\` is the inverse of the existing \`_cx_to_rx\` so clicks inside a tab's render-cells land o

prs_all.title: feat(cli): show acknowledgment when user steering hint is processed
prs_all.body_preview:
Refs #26485

## Summary

When a user submits a steering hint mid-turn, today the CLI silently
splices the hint into the conversation and the user gets no visible
feedback that the hint registered until the model itself acknowledges
it ŌĆö which can be slow, may be omitted, or per #21508 may even leak

prs_all.body_preview:
### Issue for this PR

Closes #14899

(Overlaps with the older PR #19135, which targets `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts` ŌĆö keybind defaults have since moved to `packages/opencode/src/config/keybinds.ts`, so #19135 no longer applies cleanly. This PR updates the current source-of-truth file pl

prs_all.title: fix(build): detect Bun runtime in build scripts to avoid hardcoded npm
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

`scripts/build.js`, `scripts/build_package.js`, and the husky `pre-commit` hook all invoke `npm` unconditionally. This breaks `bun install` and committing on Bun-only systems (where `npm` is not on `PATH`), because the `prepare` script chain runs `scripts/build.js` and the pre-commit hoo

prs_all.title: fix(tui): prevent question option submission on drag-to-select copy
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

In the TUI question prompt, dragging to select text on a question option (or tab) submits the option instead of copying the selected text to the clipboard. This is especially common in **plan mode** where the agent asks multiple-choice questions.

## Root cause

The app root handles copy

prs_all.title: fix: make Home/End move caret to line edges in prompt input
prs_all.body_preview: ## Summary - The prompt input is a `contenteditable` div, not a `

prs_all.title: Parallelize chunk generation across rayon worker pool
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
`Chunk::generate()` previously ran on the render thread, throttled to 8 chunks/frame in normal mode and 64 in initial loading. With `VIEW_DISTANCE=35` that's 5,041 chunks sampled serially on a single core while the rest of the CPU sat idle. This PR moves generation onto the existing rayon

prs_all.title: Fix cloud transparency over water when viewed from above
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Clouds render with `DepthMask` off and were always drawn before water, so when the camera was above the cloud layer the water draw passed depth and overwrote the cloud's color contribution ŌĆö water visible through clouds with almost no occlusion.
- Now the cloud layer is drawn last when

prs_all.title: fix: prevent status bar from wrapping when narrower than its content
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- When `left + right` is wider than `_cols` in `_draw_status_bar`, the bar was emitted in full and relied on the terminal to wrap onto a second row.
- That wrap scrolled the screen up by one line, leaving the cursor positioned on the `~` row below the intended buffer line ŌĆö the distortion

prs_all.title: fix: ensure closing fence in WrapCodeBlock is on its own line
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

`WrapCodeBlock` glued the closing ``` ``` ``` directly onto the last line of the content. When the input didn't end with a newline (e.g. a source file without a trailing newline), Markdown renderers never saw the closing fence on its own line and the code block stayed open through the re

prs_all.title: feat: POSIX recipe prefixes, -S/-e/-t flags, command-line macro overrides
prs_all.body_preview:
PR 2 of ~6 toward POSIX Make conformance. Stacked on #4 ŌåÆ #3.

## Summary

**Recipe prefixes** (any combination, any order, all stripped before expansion):
- `-` ignores the exit status of that recipe line
- `+` always runs even under `-n`/`-q`
- `@` continues to silence

**Flags:**
- `-S` cancels a

prs_all.title: feat(expand): POSIX expansion ŌĆö ${VAR}, substitution refs, $?/$*/$+
prs_all.body_preview:
First slice of the POSIX-Make conformance work. Stacked on top of #3 (`feat/parallel-jobs`) ŌĆö once that merges, GitHub will rebase this PR's base to `master`.

## Summary
- `${VAR}` curly-brace macro syntax alongside `$(VAR)`. `_parse_func` now takes the opener char and matches the corresponding clo

prs_all.title: feat: parallel jobs (-j N) and mtime-aware Makefile
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Convert `Executor` from a synchronous class into an actor; add a one-shot `_Worker` actor per recipe set. The coordinator tracks remaining-prereq counts and dispatches as workers free up, capped at `-j` (default 1).
- Refactor `Expand.with_node` ŌåÆ `Expand.with_auto` taking a sendable `A

prs_all.title: docs: update README for Pony rewrite
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Replace Rust/cargo references with `ponyc` and `make`
- Document the actual flag set from `cli.pony` (`-i`, `-s`, `-q`, `-p`, `-r`, `--version`)
- Drop the parallel-jobs claim ŌĆö `-j` is parsed but never read by the executor
- Note cycle-safe variable expansion

Follow-up to #1; the READ

prs_all.title: rewrite: port mkultra from Rust to Pony, add install target
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Replace Rust implementation with a stdlib-only Pony port (`src/{cli,parser,expand,dag,executor,main}.pony`)
- Drop Cargo manifests and stale Rust-era test fixtures
- Add `Makefile` with `all` / `clean` / `test` / `install` / `uninstall`; `install` puts the binary at `~/.local/bin/mkultr

prs_all.title: fix: SHIFT+G goes to bottom of file instead of top
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Fixed a bug where pressing `SHIFT+G` (uppercase G) in normal mode, which should move the cursor to the bottom of the file (vim behavior), instead moved it to the top.

## Root Cause

In `src/editor.pony`, the `count` variable defaults to `1` when no count prefix is typed (`let count = if

prs_all.title: feat: add ghostty config to disable ligatures
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Adds `.config/ghostty/config` with `font-feature = -calt`
- Disables contextual-alternates ligatures so ASCII characters like `*` and `#` render at correct cell width instead of being merged into ligature glyphs by programming fonts

## Test plan
- [ ] Open Ghostty, confirm `*` and `#`

prs_all.title: fix(parser,semantic): resolve crashes and get all 67 tests passing
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Fixes multiple parser and semantic analyzer bugs to get the C port test suite to 67/67 passing.

### Parser fixes
- Return statement no longer consumes semicolon twice
- For-loop init handles C99-style declarations (`for (int i = 0; ...)`) without extra semicolon consumption
- Array decl

prs_all.title: chore(deps): replace image and rayon with smaller alternatives
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Remove `image` crate; replace with direct `png` decoding
- Remove `rayon` crate; replace `rayon::spawn` with `std::thread::spawn`
- Drop `rayon-core`, `crossbeam-*`, `either`, `image`, `num-traits`, and other transitive deps

## Verification
```
cargo check
```
Passes cleanly.

prs_all.title: chore(deps): update dependencies and remove unused crates
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Remove unused dependencies: `lazy_static`, `noise`
- Update `glfw` 0.61.0 ŌåÆ 0.62.0
- Update `rand` and `rayon` to latest patch versions
- Reduce `image` crate to only `png` feature (drops unused format support)
- Fix missing trailing newline in `Cargo.toml`

## Verification
```
cargo ch

prs_all.title: fix(gitattributes): make src-c/ code detectable by linguist
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
GitHub Linguist was ignoring all C files because of a blanket `*.c linguist-detectable=false` rule in `.gitattributes`. Since `src-c/` contains the actual compiler implementation being incrementally ported from TypeScript to C, it should be counted in language statistics.

## Changes
- Ad

prs_all.title: feat(parser): implement C recursive descent parser
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Implement complete recursive descent parser in C (parser.c)
- Add lexer tests (10 tests) and parser tests (12 tests)
- Fix lexer operator tokenization: <<= and >>= must be checked before << and >>
- Fix function call argument parsing to use parse_assignment instead of parse_expression

prs_all.title: feat(port): C lexer and parser header (phase 1 of TypeScriptŌåÆC port)
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

- Adds `src-c/lexer/lexer.h` and `src-c/lexer/lexer.c` ŌĆö a complete C implementation of the tokenizer, replacing `src/lexer/Lexer.ts` (633 lines of TypeScript ŌåÆ ~450 lines of C)
- Adds `src-c/parser/parser.h` ŌĆö full AST node type definitions and parser interface, covering all node types

prs_all.title: fix(preprocessor): process directives in included files
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Extract main preprocessing loop into `preprocess_token_stream()` so `handle_include()` can recursively preprocess included files
- Previously, included files only had macro expansion applied, so `#define` directives inside headers were never executed
- Fix `compile.ts` C preprocessor in

prs_all.title: ci: switch GitHub Actions to Bun
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Fixes CI failures caused by the new CLI tests requiring Bun, while the GitHub Actions workflow was using Node.js.

### Changes
- Revert `cli.test.ts` back to `bun run src/compile.ts`
- Update CI workflow to use `oven-sh/setup-bun` instead of `actions/setup-node`
- Use `bun install`, `bun

prs_all.title: feat(cli): add gcc-compatible compiler flags
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

Adds comprehensive gcc-like CLI argument parsing to `compile.ts` and updates `pcc-wrapper` to pass flags through properly.

### New flags
- Compilation modes: `-o`, `-c`, `-S`, `-E`
- Preprocessor: `-I <path>`, `-D <name>[=value]`
- Quality/debug: `-g`, `-O0/1/2/3/s/z`, `-Wall`, `-Werror

prs_all.title: feat(substitute): add regex support to :s/ command
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Adds a pure-Pony backtracking regex engine (`src/regex.pony`, ~230 LOC)
- Wires it into `:s/`, `:%s/`, and `:.,\$s/` ŌĆö previously literal-only

## Supported syntax
- Metachars: `.` `^` `$` `*` `+` `?`
- Classes: `[abc]`, `[a-z]`, `[^...]`
- Escapes: `\d \D \w \W \s \S \n \t \r \\` + `\`

prs_all.title: feat(runtime): pure-Rust runtime crate; port point.c
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

- Introduces `crates/runtime/` ŌĆö a pure-Rust `staticlib` crate that will progressively replace the C runtime under `lib/src/` while preserving the tree-sitter C ABI.
- Ports `lib/src/point.c` as the first module. `ts_point_edit` is implemented in Rust and exported with `extern "C"` linka

prs_all.title: feat: add protected-branch check to git safety rules
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Add a rule to check for protected branches before pushing, and open a PR instead when protected.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs_all.title: fix(build): invoke tsx directly via node --import instead of npx
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
`scripts/build.js` calls `execSync('npx tsx scripts/generate-settings-schema.ts', ...)`. When the build is invoked under bun (e.g. \`bun run build\`), bun's \`npx\` wrapper intercepts the call and runs \`tsx\` inside bun's runtime, where tsx's CJS entry fails to resolve with:

\`\`\`
erro

This includes repetitive thoughts detection, read file loop detection, and action stagnation detection.

When the model generates a tool call with invalid parameters, the validation erro

prs_all.title: fix(web-search): stop leaking abort listeners in custom provider retry
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

`fetchWithRetry` in `src/tools/WebSearchTool/providers/custom.ts` attached a fresh abort listener to the caller's `AbortSignal` on every retry attempt and never removed it:

```js
signal?.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort(), { once: true })
```

`{ once: true }` only clean

prs_all.title: fix(web-search): close SSRF bypasses in custom provider hostname guard
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary

The custom search provider's `isPrivateHostname` guard in `src/tools/WebSearchTool/providers/custom.ts` missed several literal-address forms that resolve to private/reserved IPs. The most notable: **IPv4-mapped IPv6** (`[::ffff:127.0.0.1]`, which WHATWG URL normalizes to `[::ffff:7f00:1]

prs_all.title: fix: display selected model in startup screen instead of hardcoded sonnet 4.6
prs_all.body_preview:
## Summary
- Fix startup screen to display the actual selected model instead of hardcoded sonnet-4-6
- Now reads model from `settings.model` first, then falls back to `ANTHROPIC_MODEL`/`CLAUDE_MODEL` env vars
- Uses `parseUserSpecifiedModel` to resolve model aliases (e.g., `opus` ŌåÆ `claude-opus-4-6`

prs_all.title: fix(update): check version exists before confirming uninstall

When the AI is responding, slash commands like `/settings`, `/help`, `/clear` etc. were being queued and sent to the LLM as regular messages instead of being handled as CLI commands.

pull_requests.title: ci: add cppcheck security scanning workflow
pull_requests.body:
Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs cppcheck on every push and PR to catch potential bugs, performance issues, and portability problems early.

**Design decisions:**
- Scans only project source files (`catimg.c`, `sh_color.c`, `sh_image.c`, `sh_utils.c`) ŌĆö excludes the vendored `stb_image.h` header entirely
- Enables `warning,performance,portability` checks (not `style` or `information`) to avoid noise
- Uses native `--error-exitcode=1` instead of fragile grep-based output filtering
- 5 explicit suppressions, all scoped to specific files or the vendored header:
  - `memleak:src/sh_color.c:30` ŌĆö KHASH_MAP_INIT_INT macro (memory freed properly on `free_hash_colors()`)
  - `returnDanglingLifetime:src/sh_image.c` ŌĆö stb_image wrapper returns heap memory, false positive
  - `autovarInvalidDeallocation:src/sh_image.c` ŌĆö stb_image GIF code only frees heap data, false positive
  - `unusedFunction:src/stb_image.h` ŌĆö vendored header has unused internal functions
  - `staticFunction:src/stb_im

pull_requests.title: test: add unit test suite (35 tests)
pull_requests.body:
Add a unit test suite covering core library functions:

**sh_color** (15 tests)
- RGBŌåÆYUV conversion for black, white, red, green, blue
- YUV distance: same, different, symmetry
- Color copy: basic, source preservation
- Hash color init/free, palette conversion (exact, transparent, near-white, near-black)

**sh_utils** (8 tests)
- RGB/hex conversion macros (roundtrip, edge cases)
- `SQUARED` macro

**sh_image** (12 tests)
- Create, copy (deep copy verification)
- Pixel set/get, corner cases
- Resize (downscale, frame preservation)
- Color conversion, free (with/without delays)
- File loading (PNG, ICO)

**Changes from the original bundled PR:**
- Removed terminal dimension tests (fail in headless CI via `ioctl(TIOCGWINSZ)`)
- Removed `pixelToInt` tests (reimplemented a static internal function)
- All remaining tests are pure unit tests with no TTY or terminal dependencies
- Builds and runs cleanly with `ctest`

pull_requests.title: docs(man): refresh catimg.1 to match current CLI behavior
pull_requests.body:
Refresh the `catimg(1)` manual page so it reflects how the current C implementation actually works.

- Document stdin input via `-` in synopsis/arguments
- Clarify that `-w` and `-H` are mutually exclusive
- Correct `-H` semantics (remove stale '0 means terminal height' claim)
- Expand loop/resolution descriptions to match runtime behavior
- Add environment, exit status, and practical examples sections
- Update manual metadata to indicate a current revision

This is a focused PR for the documentation improvements that were previously bundled with the security hardening work.

pull_requests.title: security: hardening, add CI, and improve POSIX/Clang compliance
pull_requests.body:
This PR introduces several security hardening and code quality improvements:

- **Harden Format String Usage (CWE-134)**: Replaced `printf()` with `fputs()` for constant strings. This follows defense-in-depth best practices to prevent potential format string issues if macros are modified in the future.
- **Harden Terminal Clearing**: Replaced `system("clear")` with ANSI escape sequences (`\033[2J\033[H`). This removes a shell invocation (addressing potential PATH-based attack vectors) and improves GIF playback performance by avoiding `fork()`/`exec()`.
- **POSIX Compliance via Clang**: By preferring Clang for its superior POSIX compliance and stricter diagnostic output, several necessary improvements were identified and addressed:
    - Removed redundant local `getopt` declarations that conflicted with system headers under stricter checks.
    - Added `(void)sig` to signal handlers to satisfy compiler requirements for unused parameters.
    - Added `static` to internal functions to pro

pull_requests.title: fix: upgrade stb_image to v2.30 and improve MJPEG (AVI1)/raw YUYV decoding
pull_requests.body:
**What kind of change does this PR introduce?** (check at least one)

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Code style update
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Build-related changes
- [ ] Other, please describe:

**Does this PR introduce a breaking change?** (check one)

- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

**Other information:**

 This PR contains my 2 commits since forking:

1. Upgrade `stb_image` to v2.30 and improve MJPEG (`AVI1`) handling by loading default JPEG Huffman tables when DHT tables are missing.
2. Add fallback decoding for raw YUYV inputs in common resolutions, and improve decode error messages by printing `stbi_failure_reason()`.
